channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties with Nordic Nations
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is strengthening alliances between "middle powers" as the United States becomes a less reliable partner, part of broader efforts to counter Russian and Chinese activity in the Arctic.

item.title: 🇸🇱 Sierra Leone to Accept Hundreds of Deported West Africans from US
item.description: The deal is the latest in a series of agreements by the Trump administration to accelerate removals of undocumented immigrants from the United States.

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvia Nominates New Prime Minister After Government Resignation
item.description: President Edgars Rinkevics proposed opposition lawmaker Andris Kulbergs as the next prime minister following Evika Silina's resignation.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Tata and ASML Sign Major Semiconductor Deal During Modi Netherlands Visit
item.description: India's Tata Group and Dutch chip equipment giant ASML signed a semiconductor cooperation agreement as Prime Minister Modi visited the Netherlands, with talks also focusing on defense and security cooperation.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Young Kyiv Couple Killed in Russian Airstrike Had Hoped to Start Family
item.description: Mourners in Kyiv remembered a young couple killed in a Russian airstrike, one of many civilian casualties as Russia continues its bombardment of Ukrainian cities.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Threatens to Block All Military Equipment Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran declared it will no longer allow "enemy" military equipment through the strategic Strait of Hormuz as tensions escalate with Israel and the US, potentially disrupting global oil shipments.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Raises CNG Prices for Second Day Amid Iran War Economic Fallout
item.description: CNG prices in Delhi NCR have been hiked for the second time in two days as India faces economic impacts from the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict and global oil supply concerns.

item.title: 🇶🇦 Qatar and Saudi Arabia Discuss Efforts to Ease Middle East Tensions
item.description: Qatar's foreign minister and Saudi counterpart held phone discussions on reducing regional tensions amid the Iran-Israel conflict, reviewing bilateral ties and coordination efforts.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Gold Recycling Industry Sees Record Growth as Bullion Freney Continues
item.description: China's gold recycling sector is expanding at its fastest pace in a decade as sustained demand in the bullion market drives citizens to sell old gold for profit.

item.title: 🇳🇵 Nepali Sherpa Kami Rita Summits Everest for Record 32nd Time
item.description: Kami Rita Sherpa extended his own world record by summiting Mount Everest for the 32nd time, cementing his legendary status in mountaineering history.

item.title: 🦠 WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda International Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus in DRC and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, signaling heightened global health risk.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drone Attack Kills Four in Russia, Moscow Faces Largest Assault in Over a Year
item.description: A major Ukrainian drone attack killed at least four people across Russian regions, with Moscow experiencing its largest aerial assault in more than a year as the war intensifies.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korean Soccer Club Arrives in South Korea for Asian Champions League
item.description: Naegohyang FC became the first North Korean athletes to visit South Korea in eight years, arriving for an Asian Women's Champions League semi-final amid strained inter-Korean relations.

item.title: 🏛️ Senate Removes Funding for Trump White House Ballroom From Spending Package
item.description: A U.S. Senate official stripped security funding that could be used for President Trump's planned White House ballroom from a massive spending package, imperiling Republican efforts to use taxpayer money for the project.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Vows All Options to Prevent Samsung Strike
item.description: South Korea's prime minister said the government will pursue all options including emergency arbitration to avoid a labor strike at Samsung Electronics, the country's largest employer.

item.title: ⚓🇺🇸 USS Gerald R. Ford Returns After Record 11-Month Deployment Supporting Iran War and Venezuela Operation
item.description: The worlds largest aircraft carrier returned to Virginia after 326 days at sea, the longest deployment since Vietnam War, having supported combat operations against Iran and the capture of Venezuelas Maduro. The crew received the Presidential Unit Citation for outstanding performance in combat.

item.title: 🦠🌍 WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared a public health emergency of international concern after over 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths from the Bundibugyo virus variant, which has no approved treatments or vaccines. Cases have spread from Congo to Ugandas capital Kampala.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇸 Pentagon Cancels Europe Troop Deployment Amid Frustrations With NATO Over Iran War
item.description: The Pentagon withdrew thousands of soldiers from a planned deployment to Poland as Trump expressed anger over European allies refusal to support the war with Iran. The move signals deepening US frustration with NATO burden-sharing as the Iran conflict strains alliance relations.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE clarifies OPEC exit was strategic, not political
item.description: UAE Energy Minister Al Mazrouei said the country's withdrawal from OPEC and OPEC+ was a sovereign decision based on long-term production policy assessment, not a political move.

item.title: 🇬🇧⚔️ RAF deploys new low-cost anti-drone missile system in Middle East
item.description: British Typhoon jets in the Middle East are being equipped with new anti-drone missile systems to cost-effectively intercept Iranian drones. The system went from testing to deployment in under two months.

item.title: 💸⚔️ US approves $25.8 billion in weapons sales to Middle East allies
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio approved massive weapons sales to Middle Eastern allies affected by the Iran conflict, demonstrating continued US commitment to regional partners.

item.title: 🇦🇲🇦🇿 Armenia struggles in limbo after Nagorno-Karabakh loss
item.description: Nearly three years after Azerbaijan's lightning offensive reclaimed Nagorno-Karabakh, Armenia remains in geopolitical uncertainty, with citizens told to "be ready for war" amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK to scrap planned fuel tax rise amid economic pressures
item.description: British finance minister Rachel Reeves plans to announce postponement of a fuel tax increase scheduled for September, as the government navigates economic challenges from the Iran conflict and global inflation.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK far-right and pro-Palestine protests draw tens of thousands in London
item.description: Tommy Robinsons Unite the Kingdom march and annual Nakba Day pro-Palestine demonstration brought large crowds to central London, with police monitoring both events.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel Strikes Lebanon as Iran Says It Cannot Trust the US
item.description: Israel continues airstrikes on southern Lebanon despite a 45-day ceasefire extension, while Irans foreign minister says Tehran cannot trust the Americans at all as diplomatic efforts to end the war struggle.
